frameworks.name: population coding / neural ensemble representation
frameworks.one_line: Decision variable is carried by a population whose active members change over time.

frameworks.name: action-linked decision representation in LIP
frameworks.one_line: Single-neuron action-locked code is extended to action-invariant ensemble code.

frameworks.name: receptive field remapping
frameworks.one_line: Perisaccadic RF remapping is generalized from perception to ongoing cognition.

core_question: Can lateral intraparietal cortex sustain an evolving decision across intervening actions that disrupt the action-target linkage of individual neurons?
key_assumptions: LIP single-neuron decision signals are tied to a specific action / response field
An action that breaks the response-field linkage should disrupt the single-neuron code
Simultaneously recorded ensembles can reveal continuity invisible to single units
manipulations: Intervening actions inserted during an ongoing decision
Simultaneous multi-neuron recording in two rhesus monkeys
key_findings: Intervening actions disrupt decision representation in individual LIP neurons
The ensemble preserves the decision by handing off information from currently active to post-action neurons
Decision representation generalizes across actions and transcends the original frame of reference
interpretations: LIP carries an evolving decision via population hand-off rather than fixed single-neuron codes
Receptive-field remapping logic extends from perceptual stability to continuity of a thought process
